name: Internal Rate of Return Calculator
description: Industrial-grade IRR calculator compliant with ISO 15686, Lean, Six Sigma, and WERC standards. Computes net present value, internal rate of return, and provides actionable insights for capital investment decisions.

inputs.label: Initial Investment
inputs.businessContext: Total upfront capital expenditure (CapEx) for the project or asset.

inputs.label: Annual Cash Inflow
inputs.businessContext: Expected net cash inflow per year (revenue minus operating costs).

inputs.label: Project Life (Years)
inputs.businessContext: Duration over which cash flows are projected.

inputs.label: Discount Rate (WACC)
inputs.businessContext: Weighted average cost of capital or required rate of return.

inputs.label: Terminal Value
inputs.businessContext: Residual value at end of project life (e.g., salvage, resale).

inputs.label: Cash Flow Variability
inputs.businessContext: Risk level affecting confidence in cash flow estimates (low = ±5%, medium = ±15%, high = ±30%).

inputs.label: Lean Six Sigma Adjustment
inputs.businessContext: If enabled, applies a 2% reduction to cash flows to account for process waste and variation (Lean Six Sigma standard).

rules.message: Initial investment must be greater than zero.
Project life must be at least 1 year.
Discount rate must be between 0% and 100%.
Annual cash inflow cannot be negative.

thresholds.message: IRR is below the discount rate (WACC). Project may not meet required return.
IRR is exceptionally high (>50%). Verify cash flow assumptions for realism.
Net present value is negative. Project destroys value.
Payback period exceeds project life. Investment may not be recovered.

formulas.name: Adjusted Annual Cash Flow
formulas.description: Applies Lean Six Sigma adjustment if enabled.
formulas.formula: IF lean_six_sigma_adjustment THEN annual_cash_inflow * 0.98 ELSE annual_cash_inflow

formulas.name: Net Present Value (NPV)
formulas.description: Sum of discounted cash flows minus initial investment. ISO 15686 compliant.

formulas.name: Internal Rate of Return (IRR)
formulas.description: Discount rate that makes NPV = 0. Solved via Newton-Raphson iteration (tolerance 0.0001).
formulas.formula: IRR = r such that 0 = -initial_investment + SUM_{t=1}^{project_life_years} (adjusted_cash_flow / (1 + r)^t) + (terminal_value / (1 + r)^project_life_years)

formulas.name: Payback Period
formulas.description: Time to recover initial investment from undiscounted cash flows.

formulas.name: Discounted Payback Period
formulas.description: Time to recover initial investment from discounted cash flows.
formulas.formula: Solve for smallest n such that SUM_{t=1}^{n} (adjusted_cash_flow / (1 + discount_rate/100)^t) >= initial_investment

formulas.name: Profitability Index (PI)
formulas.description: Ratio of present value of future cash flows to initial investment.

formulas.name: Data Confidence Factor
formulas.description: Adjusts confidence based on cash flow variability and Lean Six Sigma adjustment.
formulas.formula: CASE cash_flow_variability WHEN 'low' THEN 0.95 WHEN 'medium' THEN 0.85 WHEN 'high' THEN 0.70 END * (1 - 0.02 * lean_six_sigma_adjustment)

primary.label: Internal Rate of Return
primary.description: Calculated IRR rounded to 2 decimal places.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Process Waste (Lean)
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Annual cash flow reduction due to Lean Six Sigma adjustment (2% of annual cash inflow).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Variability Risk Premium
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Expected loss due to cash flow variability (based on variability level).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Opportunity Cost of Capital
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Foregone return if capital were invested at discount rate instead.

suggestedActions.action: Consider reducing initial investment, increasing cash inflows, or extending project life.
Implement Six Sigma DMAIC to reduce cash flow variability and improve confidence.
Enable Lean Six Sigma adjustment to identify and eliminate process waste.
Run sensitivity analysis on discount rate and cash inflows to assess robustness.
